title: Bitcoin Mining Firms Sell 32,000 BTC and Sign $70B in AI Contracts in Largest Desertion Ever
summary: Companies paid to secure Bitcoin sold a record 32,000 coins and signed $70B in contracts to pivot to AI. Bitcoin's hashing power dipped briefly then recovered.
body:
The companies paid to secure Bitcoin—major mining firms—have just executed the largest desertion in the network's history. They sold a record 32,000 BTC and signed contracts worth $70 billion to walk away from Bitcoin mining and build artificial intelligence infrastructure instead.

Despite this unprecedented sell-off and pivot by key miners, Bitcoin did not flinch. Its computing power (hashrate) dipped for a moment, then climbed back to normal levels, demonstrating the network's inherent resilience.

This event highlights a major shift within the crypto-mining industry as firms chase high-growth opportunities in AI. However, the stability of Bitcoin's hashrate indicates that the network remains secure and functional even after such a large-scale exit of mining capacity.
twitterThread: The companies paid to secure Bitcoin just ran the largest desertion in its history: 32,000 coins sold, $70B in AI contracts signed. Bitcoin did not flinch.
Hashrate dipped for a moment, then climbed back. The network's resilience on full display.
If you're holding Bitcoin, this is a reminder of its stability. Don't panic. Read more → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-07-06-bitcoin-mining-firms-sell-32000-btc-to-pivot-to-ai
standaloneTweet: Bitcoin mining firms sold 32,000 BTC and signed $70B in AI deals—the largest desertion ever. Bitcoin's hashrate dipped briefly then recovered. The network stayed strong.
